## Pre-Flight Checklist System (PFCS) — Subsystem Requirements

---

### 1. Checklist Content Management Subsystem

1.1 The subsystem shall maintain a library of checklists mapped to each aircraft type and configuration operated by Meridian Regional Airways.

1.2 The subsystem shall present the flight crew with the checklist corresponding to the specific aircraft type and configuration assigned to the flight at the time of checklist initiation.

1.3 The subsystem shall distinguish mandatory checklist items from non-mandatory items, and shall enforce that distinction during checklist execution.

1.4 The subsystem shall support authorized updates to checklist content (additions, removals, and modifications to items) by personnel with appropriate administrative privileges, while preserving prior versions for historical record linkage.

---

### 2. Checklist Execution Subsystem

2.1 The subsystem shall require each checklist item to be explicitly confirmed by a crew member before the item is marked complete; passive or assumed confirmation shall not be permitted.

2.2 The subsystem shall allow a checklist item to be flagged as an exception in lieu of confirmation, and shall require the crew member to provide a documented reason before the exception is accepted.

2.3 The subsystem shall prevent issuance of a "ready for pushback" status while any mandatory checklist item remains unconfirmed and without an accepted exception or authorized override.

2.4 The subsystem shall allow a captain to authorize a documented override for one or more incomplete mandatory items, and shall record the override with the captain's identity, the item or items overridden, the stated justification, and the date and time of authorization.

2.5 The subsystem shall issue a "ready for pushback" status for a given flight only when all mandatory items are either confirmed or accounted for by an accepted exception or a captain-authorized override.

---

### 3. Crew Interaction and Role-Based Access Subsystem

3.1 The subsystem shall enforce role-based access control with distinct permission sets for at minimum the following roles: flight crew (pilot and co-pilot), captain (override authority), dispatcher, maintenance personnel, and safety reviewer.

3.2 The subsystem shall authenticate each user prior to permitting any checklist action, and shall associate every action taken with the authenticated identity of the user who performed it.

3.3 The subsystem shall restrict checklist item confirmation and exception flagging to authenticated flight crew members assigned to the flight.

3.4 The subsystem shall restrict override authorization to authenticated captains assigned to the flight.

3.5 The subsystem shall restrict dispatch release actions to authenticated dispatchers.

3.6 The subsystem shall provide safety reviewers with read-only access to completed checklist records and shall prevent safety reviewers from modifying any record.

3.7 The subsystem shall prevent any user from accessing checklist data for flights to which they are not assigned, except dispatchers and safety reviewers accessing records within their operational scope.

---

### 4. Audit and Records Subsystem

4.1 The subsystem shall record the identity of the crew member who confirmed or flagged each checklist item and the date and time at which each action was taken.

4.2 The subsystem shall retain a complete checklist record for each flight, including all item confirmations, exceptions with stated reasons, and any overrides with their justifications and authorizing captain.

4.3 The subsystem shall produce tamper-evident checklist records such that any post-completion alteration to a record is detectable by operations and safety staff.

4.4 The subsystem shall retain checklist records for a minimum retention period as defined by Meridian Regional Airways operational and regulatory policy, and shall make records available for retrieval by authorized operations and safety staff.

4.5 The subsystem shall provide operations and safety staff with the ability to search and retrieve historical checklist records by flight identifier, date, aircraft, and crew member.

---

### 5. Dispatch Integration Subsystem

5.1 The subsystem shall provide an interface to the Meridian Regional Airways dispatch system through which PFCS communicates the checklist completion status for each flight.

5.2 The subsystem shall withhold a "ready for pushback" release signal to the dispatch system for any flight whose checklist status is not complete as defined in Checklist Execution Subsystem requirement 2.5.

5.3 The dispatch system integration shall ensure that a flight cannot be released for pushback unless PFCS has transmitted a confirmed "ready for pushback" status for that flight.

5.4 The subsystem shall transmit an alert to the assigned dispatcher whenever a captain authorizes an override on that dispatcher's flight.

5.5 The subsystem shall transmit an alert to the assigned dispatcher whenever a mandatory checklist item is flagged as an exception on that dispatcher's flight.

5.6 The subsystem shall log all status transmissions and alerts sent to the dispatch system, including timestamp and acknowledgment state, as part of the flight's audit record.

---

### 6. Offline Operation and Data Reconciliation Subsystem

6.1 The subsystem shall remain fully functional for checklist execution, item confirmation, exception flagging, override authorization, and status determination when the device has no network connectivity.

6.2 The subsystem shall store all checklist actions, records, and status changes generated during offline operation in local persistent storage on the device.

6.3 The subsystem shall detect the restoration of network connectivity and automatically initiate synchronization of locally stored records with the central data store without requiring crew intervention.

6.4 The subsystem shall resolve conflicts arising from concurrent offline activity (e.g., records generated on multiple devices for the same flight) according to defined reconciliation rules that preserve a complete and accurate audit trail without data loss.

6.5 The subsystem shall provide an indication to the user of current connectivity state and synchronization status.

6.6 The subsystem shall ensure that reconciled records written to the central store satisfy the tamper-evidence requirements of the Audit and Records Subsystem.

## Subsystem Requirements

---

### 1. Checklist Content and Configuration Subsystem

1.1 The subsystem shall maintain a library of checklists mapped to each aircraft type and configuration operated by Meridian Regional Airways.

1.2 The subsystem shall automatically select and present the correct checklist based on the aircraft type and configuration assigned to a given flight.

1.3 The subsystem shall distinguish between mandatory checklist items and non-mandatory checklist items.

1.4 The subsystem shall support versioning of checklist content so that updates to checklist definitions do not alter records of previously completed flights.

1.5 The subsystem shall allow authorized personnel (e.g., safety or standards staff) to create, modify, and retire checklist definitions through a controlled change process.

---

### 2. Checklist Execution Subsystem

2.1 The subsystem shall present each checklist item sequentially or in the defined order to the flight crew during pre-flight operations.

2.2 The subsystem shall require each checklist item to be explicitly confirmed by a crew member before it is marked complete.

2.3 The subsystem shall allow a checklist item to be flagged as an exception, and shall require the crew member to provide a documented reason when flagging any item.

2.4 The subsystem shall prevent progression to a "ready for pushback" status if any mandatory item remains unconfirmed and has not been authorized as an override.

2.5 The subsystem shall allow a captain to authorize a documented override for a mandatory item that cannot be confirmed, and shall record the override authorization against the captain's identity and timestamp.

2.6 The subsystem shall capture and associate the identity of the crew member completing each checklist item with the corresponding item record, along with the date and time of completion.

---

### 3. Pushback Authorization Subsystem

3.1 The subsystem shall compute and maintain a "ready for pushback" status for each flight, setting that status only when all mandatory checklist items are either confirmed or covered by a captain-authorized, documented override.

3.2 The subsystem shall withhold "ready for pushback" status for any flight that has one or more unresolved mandatory items without a valid override.

3.3 The subsystem shall expose the "ready for pushback" status to the dispatch integration subsystem in a form that the dispatch system can consume to gate the release of a flight for pushback.

3.4 The subsystem shall log every transition of pushback status, including the conditions under which the status was granted.

---

### 4. Dispatch Integration Subsystem

4.1 The subsystem shall provide an interface to the Meridian Regional Airways dispatch system through which PFCS transmits the pushback-readiness status of each flight.

4.2 The subsystem shall ensure that the dispatch system cannot record a flight as released for pushback unless PFCS has reported that flight's checklist as complete (all mandatory items confirmed or validly overridden).

4.3 The subsystem shall synchronize flight release data between PFCS and the dispatch system, and shall reconcile any discrepancies that arise from offline operation once connectivity is restored.

4.4 The subsystem shall handle dispatch system unavailability gracefully, queuing status updates for delivery once the connection is re-established without blocking local checklist execution.

---

### 5. Alerting and Notification Subsystem

5.1 The subsystem shall generate an alert to the assigned dispatcher whenever a captain authorizes an override on a mandatory checklist item.

5.2 The subsystem shall generate an alert to the assigned dispatcher whenever any mandatory checklist item is flagged as an exception.

5.3 The subsystem shall deliver alerts through a defined notification channel and shall record the time each alert was generated and the dispatcher to whom it was directed.

5.4 The subsystem shall provide confirmation or acknowledgment tracking so that unacknowledged alerts can be identified and escalated if necessary.

---

### 6. Audit and Records Subsystem

6.1 The subsystem shall create and retain a complete checklist record for every flight, capturing item-level confirmation or exception status, the identity of the confirming crew member, timestamps, override authorizations, and final pushback status.

6.2 The subsystem shall store all checklist records in a tamper-evident format such that any unauthorized modification or deletion of a record can be detected.

6.3 The subsystem shall retain checklist records for a retention period defined by Meridian Regional Airways operations and safety policy.

6.4 The subsystem shall provide query and reporting interfaces that allow authorized operations and safety staff to retrieve, review, and export checklist records for specified flights or date ranges.

6.5 The subsystem shall record the source and reconciliation status of each record so that records created offline can be distinguished from those created with live connectivity until reconciliation is confirmed.

---

### 7. Role-Based Access Control Subsystem

7.1 The subsystem shall define and enforce distinct access roles for at minimum the following user categories: flight crew, captain (as a distinct privilege level within flight crew), dispatcher, maintenance, and safety reviewer.

7.2 The subsystem shall restrict checklist item confirmation and exception flagging to authenticated flight crew members assigned to the relevant flight.

7.3 The subsystem shall restrict override authorization to authenticated captains assigned to the relevant flight.

7.4 The subsystem shall restrict checklist record review and reporting functions to authenticated operations and safety reviewer roles.

7.5 The subsystem shall restrict checklist content management functions to authorized standards or safety personnel roles.

7.6 The subsystem shall authenticate all users before granting access to any PFCS function, and shall log all authentication events, including failures.

7.7 The subsystem shall enforce role-based permissions consistently regardless of whether the system is operating online or offline.

---

### 8. Offline Operation and Data Synchronization Subsystem

8.1 The subsystem shall allow flight crew to execute the full checklist workflow — including item confirmation, exception flagging, override authorization, and pushback status generation — without any dependency on network connectivity.

8.2 The subsystem shall store all checklist data created during offline operation locally on the device in a manner that preserves integrity and tamper-evidence.

8.3 The subsystem shall detect when network connectivity to the central data store is restored and shall automatically initiate reconciliation of locally stored records with the central store.

8.4 The subsystem shall resolve data conflicts arising from concurrent offline updates according to defined reconciliation rules, and shall flag any conflicts that cannot be resolved automatically for manual review.

8.5 The subsystem shall ensure that reconciliation does not result in data loss, duplication, or corruption of any checklist record.

8.6 The subsystem shall provide status indicators to users showing whether the system is operating in online or offline mode and whether any pending records have not yet been synchronized.

7.3 The subsystem shall detect restoration of network connectivity and automatically initiate reconciliation of locally stored records with the central data store.

8.2 The subsystem shall restrict checklist item confirmation and exception flagging to authenticated users holding the flight crew role.

8.7 The subsystem shall authenticate each user prior to granting access and shall enforce role permissions consistently during both online and offline operation.

1.1 The subsystem shall maintain a library of checklists indexed by aircraft type, variant, and configuration (e.g., MEL deferrals, equipment fit).

1.2 The subsystem shall present the flight crew with the checklist version that matches the specific aircraft type and configuration assigned to the flight.

1.3 The subsystem shall ensure that checklist content is version-controlled, and that any revision is traceable with an effective date and authoring identity.

1.4 The subsystem shall prevent a superseded checklist version from being initiated for a new flight once a newer version is in effect.

---

### 2. Checklist Execution Subsystem

2.1 The subsystem shall present each checklist item to the flight crew sequentially and require an explicit confirmation action (e.g., button press, acknowledgement gesture) for each item before it is marked complete.

2.2 The subsystem shall allow a checklist item to be flagged as an exception in lieu of confirmation, and shall require the crew member to select or enter a reason before the exception is accepted.

2.3 The subsystem shall record the identity of the crew member who confirmed or flagged each item, and the date and time at which the action was taken.

2.4 The subsystem shall prevent advancement past a mandatory item unless that item has been either confirmed or flagged with a reason.

2.5 The subsystem shall distinguish between mandatory and advisory checklist items, applying completion enforcement only to mandatory items.

---

### 3. Pushback Authorization Subsystem

3.1 The subsystem shall withhold "ready for pushback" status until every mandatory checklist item for the assigned flight has been confirmed or flagged as an exception with a reason.

3.2 The subsystem shall allow a captain to authorize a documented override that grants "ready for pushback" status when one or more mandatory items remain unconfirmed, provided the override includes a recorded justification and the captain's authenticated identity.

3.3 The subsystem shall transmit a "checklist complete" or "override authorized" signal to the dispatch system as the sole precondition for dispatch release, and shall not transmit that signal until the conditions in 3.1 or 3.2 are satisfied.

3.4 The subsystem shall block reuse or transfer of a "ready for pushback" status to a different flight or tail number.

---

### 4. Dispatch Integration Subsystem

4.1 The subsystem shall provide a defined interface (e.g., API or message exchange) through which the dispatch system queries PFCS for the pushback-authorization status of a specific flight.

4.2 The subsystem shall ensure the dispatch system cannot issue a pushback release for a flight unless PFCS has reported a valid "checklist complete" or "captain override authorized" status for that flight.

4.3 The subsystem shall communicate status updates to the dispatch system in near-real time when connectivity is available, and shall queue and deliver those updates as soon as connectivity is restored when it is not.

4.4 The subsystem shall provide a mechanism for the dispatch system to signal PFCS when a flight's departure details change (e.g., aircraft swap), triggering re-evaluation of checklist applicability.

---

### 5. Alerting and Notification Subsystem

5.1 The subsystem shall generate an alert to the responsible dispatcher whenever a captain authorizes an override on a flight within that dispatcher's scope.

5.2 The subsystem shall generate an alert to the responsible dispatcher whenever a mandatory checklist item is flagged as an exception on a flight within that dispatcher's scope.

5.3 Alerts shall include, at minimum: flight identifier, tail number, item description, crew member identity, timestamp, and the reason provided for the exception or override.

5.4 The subsystem shall deliver alerts through at least one dispatcher-facing channel (e.g., dispatch workstation notification, message queue) and shall log each alert with a delivery timestamp.

5.5 The subsystem shall queue alerts generated while connectivity is unavailable and deliver them upon restoration of connectivity without loss.

---

### 6. Audit and Record Retention Subsystem

6.1 The subsystem shall create a complete, time-stamped checklist record for each flight, capturing every item confirmation, every exception flag and its reason, and every override authorization and its justification.

6.2 The subsystem shall store records in a tamper-evident manner such that any alteration or deletion attempt is detectable (e.g., cryptographic hashing, append-only audit log).

6.3 The subsystem shall retain completed checklist records for a minimum period defined by Meridian Regional Airways operations policy and applicable regulatory requirements.

6.4 The subsystem shall make completed checklist records searchable and retrievable by authorized operations and safety staff, filtered by at least flight identifier, date range, aircraft tail number, and crew member.

6.5 The subsystem shall record the identity and timestamp of any user who accesses a completed checklist record.

---

### 7. Role-Based Access Control Subsystem

7.1 The subsystem shall define and enforce distinct access roles for, at minimum: flight crew (pilots), dispatchers, maintenance personnel, and safety reviewers.

7.2 The subsystem shall grant flight crew the ability to execute checklists, confirm items, flag exceptions, and — for captains — authorize overrides, and shall restrict them from modifying records or accessing other crews' flights except as operationally necessary.

7.3 The subsystem shall grant dispatchers the ability to view pushback-authorization status, receive alerts, and read completed checklist records for flights within their operational scope, without the ability to alter checklist execution records.

7.4 The subsystem shall grant maintenance personnel access to checklist records and exception flags relevant to aircraft airworthiness items, scoped to the aircraft within their assignment.

7.5 The subsystem shall grant safety reviewers read-only access to all completed checklist records and audit logs within their defined scope.

7.6 The subsystem shall authenticate each user before granting role-based access, and shall log all login events, role assignments, and privilege changes.

7.7 The subsystem shall prevent any user from performing actions outside the permissions defined for their assigned role(s).

---

### 8. Offline Operation and Data Synchronization Subsystem

8.1 The subsystem shall remain fully functional for checklist execution, item confirmation, exception flagging, override authorization, and local record storage when no network connectivity is available.

8.2 The subsystem shall store all checklist data generated during offline operation in persistent local storage such that data is not lost in the event of device restart or unexpected power loss.

8.3 The subsystem shall automatically detect the restoration of network connectivity and initiate synchronization of locally stored records with the central data store without requiring crew action.

8.4 The subsystem shall resolve conflicts arising from concurrent offline edits (e.g., two devices updating the same flight record) according to a defined, documented conflict-resolution policy that preserves the completeness of the audit trail.

8.5 The subsystem shall provide the user with a visible indication of current connectivity status and synchronization state (e.g., pending sync, synchronized, conflict detected).

8.6 The subsystem shall ensure that a "ready for pushback" status generated offline is transmitted to the dispatch system and the central record store upon the next successful connectivity event, and that the dispatch interface reflects that status as soon as it is received.

## Pre-Flight Checklist System (PFCS) — Subsystem Requirements

---

### 1. Checklist Content Management Subsystem

1.1 The subsystem shall maintain a library of checklists mapped to each aircraft type and configuration variant operated by Meridian Regional Airways.

1.2 The subsystem shall serve the correct checklist to the flight crew based on the assigned aircraft type and configuration for the specific flight.

1.3 The subsystem shall distinguish mandatory checklist items from non-mandatory items and enforce that distinction at runtime.

1.4 The subsystem shall allow authorized personnel (e.g., operations or safety staff) to create, revise, and retire checklist definitions, with changes versioned and audit-logged.

1.5 Each checklist version in use at the time of a flight shall be preserved alongside the completion record so that reviewers can reconstruct exactly what was presented to the crew.

---

### 2. Checklist Execution Subsystem

2.1 The subsystem shall present checklist items to the flight crew one at a time or in a structured sequence appropriate to normal crew workflow.

2.2 The subsystem shall require each checklist item to be explicitly confirmed as complete before it is marked done; passive or implicit confirmation shall not be accepted.

2.3 The subsystem shall allow a checklist item to be flagged as an exception rather than confirmed, and shall require the crew member to provide a reason before the flag is accepted.

2.4 The subsystem shall track the completion state of every item throughout the checklist session and prevent progression to "ready for pushback" status while any mandatory item remains unconfirmed and without an authorized disposition.

2.5 The subsystem shall record the identity of the crew member who confirmed or flagged each item and the timestamp at which the action was taken.

---

### 3. Override Authorization Subsystem

3.1 The subsystem shall provide a mechanism by which a captain may authorize an override for one or more mandatory items that cannot be confirmed under prevailing conditions.

3.2 The subsystem shall require the captain to provide a documented justification for each override before the override is accepted.

3.3 The subsystem shall verify that the user authorizing the override holds the captain role before accepting the action.

3.4 The subsystem shall record the identity of the authorizing captain, the item(s) overridden, the stated justification, and the exact timestamp of each override action.

3.5 The subsystem shall permit "ready for pushback" status to be issued once all mandatory items are either confirmed or covered by a captain-authorized, documented override.

---

### 4. Tamper-Evident Record Subsystem

4.1 The subsystem shall create a complete, structured record for each flight encompassing every item confirmation, exception flag, and override, together with the associated crew member identities, timestamps, checklist version, and aircraft assignment.

4.2 The subsystem shall apply a tamper-evident mechanism (e.g., cryptographic hashing or digital signing) to each finalized flight checklist record such that any subsequent alteration is detectable.

4.3 The subsystem shall retain finalized flight checklist records for the retention period defined by Meridian operations and safety policy.

4.4 The subsystem shall make retained records available for query and review by authorized operations and safety staff without permitting those staff to modify the records.

---

### 5. Dispatch Integration Subsystem

5.1 The subsystem shall expose a programmatic interface through which the dispatch system can query the pushback-readiness status of a specific flight.

5.2 The subsystem shall report a flight as ready for pushback only when the checklist is complete as defined in Requirement 3.5; it shall report the flight as not ready in all other states.

5.3 The subsystem shall ensure that the dispatch system cannot release a flight for pushback unless the PFCS interface returns a confirmed ready-for-pushback status for that flight.

5.4 The subsystem shall transmit the final checklist completion status to the dispatch system in a timely manner upon checklist closure, and shall provide a mechanism for the dispatch system to re-query status on demand.

---

### 6. Alerting Subsystem

6.1 The subsystem shall generate and deliver an alert to the assigned dispatcher whenever a captain authorizes an override on any mandatory checklist item.

6.2 The subsystem shall generate and deliver an alert to the assigned dispatcher whenever any mandatory checklist item is flagged as an exception.

6.3 Alerts shall include, at minimum, the flight identifier, the affected checklist item, the type of event (override or exception flag), the crew member involved, and the time of the event.

6.4 The subsystem shall queue alerts generated while connectivity is unavailable and deliver them automatically once connectivity is restored, with no alert permanently lost due to an offline period.

---

### 7. Role-Based Access Control Subsystem

7.1 The subsystem shall define and enforce distinct access roles for, at minimum, flight crew (pilot/first officer), captain, dispatcher, maintenance personnel, and safety reviewer.

7.2 The subsystem shall restrict checklist item confirmation and exception flagging to authenticated users holding a flight crew or captain role assigned to the relevant flight.

7.3 The subsystem shall restrict override authorization to authenticated users holding the captain role assigned to the relevant flight.

7.4 The subsystem shall restrict dispatch-status query and release actions to authenticated users holding the dispatcher role.

7.5 The subsystem shall restrict read-only access to completed flight checklist records for review purposes to authenticated users holding the safety reviewer or operations staff roles.

7.6 The subsystem shall prevent any user from accessing or acting upon a checklist for an aircraft or flight to which they are not assigned, except where review roles explicitly permit broader read access.

7.7 The subsystem shall log all authentication events and access-control decisions for audit purposes.

---

### 8. Offline Operation and Data Reconciliation Subsystem

8.1 The subsystem shall remain fully operational for checklist presentation, item confirmation, exception flagging, override authorization, status management, and local record creation when no network connectivity is available.

8.2 The subsystem shall store all actions taken during an offline period in a local, durable data store on the crew-facing device such that data is not lost if the device is restarted or if connectivity is delayed.

8.3 The subsystem shall detect when network connectivity is restored and automatically initiate synchronization of locally stored records with the central data store.

8.4 The subsystem shall resolve conflicts arising from concurrent offline activity according to defined reconciliation rules, preserving the complete history of all actions and flagging any conflict that cannot be resolved automatically for manual review.

8.5 The subsystem shall apply tamper-evident protections to locally stored records during the offline period so that integrity can be verified upon upload to the central store.

8.6 The subsystem shall notify dispatchers and operations staff of any delay in receiving checklist records due to an extended offline period, once connectivity is restored and reconciliation is complete.

2.3 The subsystem shall prevent progression to "ready for pushback" status while any mandatory item remains unconfirmed and without an authorized exception or override.

2.4 The subsystem shall record the identity of the crew member who confirmed or flagged each individual checklist item.

---

3.2 The subsystem shall require the captain to provide documented justification at the time an override is authorized.

3.3 The subsystem shall record the identity of the captain, the time of authorization, and the justification for every override.

3.4 The subsystem shall immediately notify the assigned dispatcher upon authorization of any override.

---

### 4. Pushback Readiness Status Subsystem

4.1 The subsystem shall compute and maintain a "ready for pushback" status for each flight based on the completion state of all mandatory checklist items.

4.2 The subsystem shall set "ready for pushback" status to affirmative only when all mandatory items are confirmed, or when all remaining incomplete mandatory items are covered by a captain-authorized and documented override.

4.3 The subsystem shall expose the current pushback readiness status to the dispatch integration subsystem in a format suitable for automated release decisions.

---

### 5. Dispatch Integration Subsystem

5.1 The subsystem shall interface with the Meridian Regional Airways dispatch system to report the pushback readiness status of each flight.

5.2 The subsystem shall prevent the dispatch system from releasing a flight for pushback unless the PFCS reports a confirmed "ready for pushback" status for that flight.

5.3 The subsystem shall transmit pushback readiness status updates to the dispatch system in near-real time when connectivity is available, and shall queue and deliver updates upon restoration of connectivity when operating offline.

5.4 The subsystem shall provide the dispatch system with a reference identifier linking each pushback release to the corresponding completed checklist record.

---

### 6. Alerting and Notification Subsystem

6.1 The subsystem shall generate and deliver an alert to the assigned dispatcher whenever a captain authorizes an override for a flight.

6.2 The subsystem shall generate and deliver an alert to the assigned dispatcher whenever a mandatory checklist item is flagged as an exception.

6.3 The subsystem shall deliver alerts through a channel that is functional during both online and offline operating modes, queuing alerts for delivery upon connectivity restoration where necessary.

6.4 The subsystem shall record each alert, including the recipient, content, and delivery timestamp, as part of the tamper-evident record for the associated flight.

---

### 7. Record Retention and Tamper-Evidence Subsystem

7.1 The subsystem shall create a complete, immutable record for each flight's checklist, capturing all item confirmations, exceptions, overrides, crew identities, and timestamps.

7.2 The subsystem shall protect stored records against unauthorized alteration using a tamper-evident mechanism such that any modification attempt is detectable.

7.3 The subsystem shall retain checklist records for each flight and make them available for review by authorized operations and safety staff for a retention period consistent with Meridian Regional Airways' safety record-keeping policy.

7.4 The subsystem shall provide operations and safety staff with the ability to retrieve, filter, and review historical checklist records by flight, date, aircraft, crew member, or exception and override status.

---

### 8. Role-Based Access Control Subsystem

8.2 The subsystem shall restrict checklist item confirmation and flagging to users authenticated in the flight crew role for the assigned flight.

8.3 The subsystem shall restrict override authorization to users authenticated in the captain role for the assigned flight.

8.4 The subsystem shall restrict pushback release actions within the PFCS integration to users authenticated in the dispatcher role.

8.5 The subsystem shall restrict access to historical checklist records for review purposes to users authenticated in the operations, safety reviewer, or authorized management roles.

8.6 The subsystem shall authenticate all users prior to granting role-based access and shall log all access events.

---

### 9. Offline Operation and Data Synchronization Subsystem

9.1 The subsystem shall remain fully operational for checklist execution, item confirmation, exception flagging, override authorization, and status computation when network connectivity to the central data store is unavailable.

9.2 The subsystem shall store all locally generated data — including completed items, exceptions, overrides, alerts, and status changes — on the local device in a durable manner during offline operation.

9.3 The subsystem shall detect the restoration of network connectivity and automatically initiate reconciliation of locally stored records with the central data store without requiring manual intervention from the crew.

9.4 The subsystem shall resolve data conflicts arising from concurrent offline activity according to defined and documented reconciliation rules that preserve the integrity and completeness of checklist records.

9.5 The subsystem shall provide confirmation to the user once offline data has been successfully synchronized to the central store.

9.6 The subsystem shall ensure that no locally stored checklist data is lost or corrupted during a connectivity outage or during the synchronization process.

---

### 1. Checklist Content & Configuration Management Subsystem

1.2 The subsystem shall present the correct checklist to the flight crew automatically based on the assigned aircraft type and configuration for the specific flight.

1.3 The subsystem shall allow authorized personnel to create, revise, and retire checklist versions, with each version uniquely identified and timestamped.

1.4 The subsystem shall ensure that only the current approved checklist version is presented to flight crew for active flights, while prior versions remain accessible for historical review.

1.5 The subsystem shall distinguish between mandatory checklist items and non-mandatory items, and shall enforce this classification throughout checklist execution.

---

### 2. Checklist Execution & Item Confirmation Subsystem

2.1 The subsystem shall require each checklist item to be explicitly confirmed by a crew member before it is recorded as complete; implicit or bulk completion shall not be permitted.

2.2 The subsystem shall allow a checklist item to be flagged as an exception in lieu of confirmation, and shall require the crew member to enter a reason before the exception is accepted.

2.3 The subsystem shall prevent advancement to "ready for pushback" status while any mandatory item remains unconfirmed and without an authorized exception or override.

2.4 The subsystem shall capture and associate the identity of the crew member and the timestamp with each item confirmation or exception flag at the moment of entry.

2.5 The subsystem shall support a captain-authorized override that permits "ready for pushback" status to be granted when one or more mandatory items are incomplete, subject to the override being documented with the authorizing captain's identity, timestamp, and stated justification.

---

### 3. Pushback Release & Dispatch Integration Subsystem

3.1 The subsystem shall expose a pushback-readiness status for each flight that the dispatch system can query or receive in real time.

3.2 The subsystem shall report a flight's status as "not ready for pushback" to the dispatch system whenever any mandatory checklist item is unresolved and no valid captain override is recorded.

3.3 The subsystem shall report a flight's status as "ready for pushback" to the dispatch system only when all mandatory items are confirmed, or when a documented captain override is in place.

3.4 The subsystem shall prevent the dispatch system from issuing a pushback release for any flight that PFCS has not reported as ready, and shall define and implement the interface protocol necessary to enforce this gate.

3.5 The subsystem shall transmit pushback-readiness status updates to the dispatch system within a defined latency threshold sufficient to prevent operational delays at the gate.

---

### 4. Alerting & Notification Subsystem

4.1 The subsystem shall generate an alert to the responsible dispatcher immediately whenever a captain-authorized override is recorded on any flight.

4.2 The subsystem shall generate an alert to the responsible dispatcher immediately whenever a mandatory checklist item is flagged as an exception on any active flight.

4.3 The subsystem shall deliver alerts through at least one confirmed delivery channel and shall log the alert, its intended recipient, and the delivery timestamp.

4.4 The subsystem shall allow dispatchers to acknowledge alerts, and shall record the acknowledgment with the dispatcher's identity and timestamp.

4.5 The subsystem shall escalate unacknowledged alerts after a configurable timeout period.

---

### 5. Record Keeping & Tamper-Evidence Subsystem

5.1 The subsystem shall create a complete, immutable record for each flight's checklist session, capturing every item confirmation, exception, override, crew identity, and timestamp.

5.3 The subsystem shall retain completed checklist records for a minimum retention period defined by Meridian Regional Airways and applicable regulatory requirements.

5.4 The subsystem shall provide operations and safety staff with the ability to retrieve and review individual flight checklist records, including all exceptions and overrides, without modifying those records.

5.5 The subsystem shall maintain a separate, append-only audit log of all access to stored checklist records, capturing the accessor's identity, role, and timestamp.

---

### 6. Role-Based Access Control Subsystem

6.1 The subsystem shall define and enforce discrete access roles for at minimum the following user classes: flight crew (captain and first officer), dispatcher, maintenance personnel, and safety reviewer.

6.2 The subsystem shall restrict checklist item confirmation and exception flagging to authenticated flight crew assigned to the specific flight.

6.3 The subsystem shall restrict captain override authorization to authenticated crew members holding the captain role on the specific flight.

6.4 The subsystem shall restrict pushback release actions within PFCS to authenticated dispatchers.

6.5 The subsystem shall restrict read access to completed checklist records to authorized operations, safety, and maintenance personnel according to their defined role permissions.

6.6 The subsystem shall deny all actions that fall outside a user's defined role, and shall log each denied access attempt with the user's identity, the action attempted, and the timestamp.

6.7 The subsystem shall authenticate all users before granting any system access, and shall support integration with Meridian Regional Airways' existing identity management infrastructure.

---

### 7. Offline Operation & Data Reconciliation Subsystem

7.1 The subsystem shall remain fully operational for checklist execution, item confirmation, exception flagging, override authorization, and local record storage when no network connectivity is available.

7.2 The subsystem shall locally persist all checklist session data, alerts, and audit events generated during an offline period in a durable store that survives device restart or power loss.

7.3 The subsystem shall automatically detect restoration of network connectivity and initiate synchronization of all locally stored records with the central data store without requiring manual crew intervention.

7.4 The subsystem shall resolve conflicts arising from concurrent offline activity — such as the same flight record modified on multiple devices — according to defined conflict-resolution rules that preserve data integrity and audit completeness.

7.5 The subsystem shall propagate any pushback-readiness status changes accumulated during an offline period to the dispatch integration subsystem upon reconnection, and shall flag to dispatchers any flights whose status changed while connectivity was unavailable.

7.6 The subsystem shall provide operations staff with visibility into which records were created or modified offline and subsequently reconciled, including the reconciliation timestamp.

**PFCS-CECS-02:** The subsystem shall require that any checklist item not confirmed as satisfactory be flagged as an exception, and shall require the crew member to enter a reason before the exception is accepted.

**PFCS-CECS-04:** The subsystem shall withhold "ready for pushback" status until all mandatory checklist items have been either confirmed or covered by a captain-authorized override.

**PFCS-ACRM-01:** The subsystem shall enforce role-based access control distinguishing at minimum the following roles: flight crew (captain and first officer), dispatcher, maintenance personnel, and safety reviewer.

**PFCS-ANS-03:** Alerts shall be delivered in a timely manner such that dispatchers receive notification before pushback clearance is requested, where connectivity permits, and shall be queued for immediate delivery upon connectivity restoration when operating offline.

**PFCS-DIS-03:** The dispatch system integration shall ensure that a flight release for pushback cannot be issued by the dispatch system unless a confirmed "checklist complete" status has been received from PFCS for that flight.

**PFCS-RSTE-03:** The subsystem shall retain completed flight checklist records for a period defined by Meridian Regional Airways' records retention policy and shall make them retrievable by authorized operations and safety reviewer roles.

**PFCS-RSTE-04:** The subsystem shall provide an audit trail of all access to stored records, including the identity of the user, the record accessed, and the time of access.

---

**PFCS-ODRS-01:** The subsystem shall remain fully operational for checklist execution, item confirmation, exception flagging, override authorization, and local record creation when no network connectivity is available.

**PFCS-ODRS-02:** The subsystem shall store all checklist data, actions, and records created during offline operation in a persistent local store that is protected against data loss from application or device restart.

**PFCS-ODRS-03:** Upon restoration of network connectivity, the subsystem shall automatically synchronize all locally stored records and status updates to the central data store without requiring manual crew intervention.

**PFCS-ODRS-04:** The reconciliation process shall detect and resolve or escalate conflicts between locally stored offline records and central store records, and shall preserve the integrity and completeness of the tamper-evident record during synchronization.

**PFCS-ODRS-05:** The subsystem shall provide the crew with a clear indication of current connectivity status and of the synchronization state of locally held records.

5.2 The subsystem shall protect records using a tamper-evident mechanism such that any unauthorized modification to a stored record is detectable.

5.3 The subsystem shall retain each flight checklist record for a minimum retention period as defined by Meridian Regional Airways' safety and regulatory obligations.

5.4 The subsystem shall provide authorized operations and safety staff with the ability to retrieve, view, and export historical checklist records for a specified flight or date range.

5.5 The subsystem shall ensure that no record can be deleted or altered by flight crew, dispatchers, or maintenance personnel after the checklist session for that flight is closed.

---

### 6. Dispatch System Integration Subsystem

6.1 The subsystem shall expose an interface to the dispatch system that reports the current checklist completion status for each flight.

6.2 The subsystem shall prevent the dispatch system from releasing a flight for pushback unless PFCS has reported a "ready for pushback" status for that flight.

6.3 The subsystem shall communicate status updates to the dispatch system in near-real-time when connectivity is available, and shall queue and transmit pending updates as soon as connectivity is restored following an offline period.

6.4 The subsystem shall handle dispatch system unavailability without corrupting or losing PFCS records, and shall resynchronize state with the dispatch system upon reconnection.

---

### 7. Role-Based Access Control Subsystem

7.1 The subsystem shall define and enforce distinct access roles for at minimum the following user classes: flight crew, dispatchers, maintenance personnel, and safety reviewers.

7.2 The subsystem shall restrict checklist item confirmation and exception flagging to authenticated flight crew assigned to the specific flight.

7.3 The subsystem shall restrict override authorization to users holding a captain role on the assigned flight.

7.4 The subsystem shall restrict dispatch release actions to authenticated dispatchers.

7.5 The subsystem shall restrict access to historical checklist records to operations staff, safety reviewers, and other roles explicitly granted read access, and shall prevent flight crew and dispatchers from modifying closed records.

7.6 The subsystem shall authenticate every user before granting access to any PFCS function and shall log all authentication events.

---

### 8. Offline Operation and Data Synchronization Subsystem

8.1 The subsystem shall remain fully functional for checklist execution, exception flagging, override authorization, and local record creation when no network connectivity is available.

8.2 The subsystem shall store all checklist actions and records locally on the device during offline operation using a format consistent with the central data store schema.

8.3 The subsystem shall detect the restoration of network connectivity and automatically initiate synchronization of locally stored records with the central data store.

8.4 The subsystem shall resolve data conflicts arising from concurrent offline activity on multiple devices according to a defined, deterministic conflict-resolution policy, and shall flag any conflict that cannot be automatically resolved for manual review by an authorized administrator.

8.5 The subsystem shall guarantee that no confirmed item, exception, override, or alert record created during offline operation is lost during or after the synchronization process.

8.6 The subsystem shall provide an indication to the user of current connectivity status and synchronization state at all times during system use.

## PFCS Subsystem Requirements

---

### 1. Checklist Content Management Subsystem

1.1 The subsystem shall maintain a library of checklists mapped to each aircraft type and configuration variant operated by Meridian Regional Airways.

1.2 The subsystem shall automatically select and present the correct checklist version based on the aircraft identifier and configuration data provided at the time of checklist initiation.

1.3 The subsystem shall distinguish between mandatory items and non-mandatory items within each checklist.

1.4 The subsystem shall support versioning of checklist content so that updates to checklist definitions do not alter records of previously completed checklists.

1.5 The subsystem shall prevent a checklist from being initiated if no valid checklist definition exists for the identified aircraft type and configuration.

---

### 2. Checklist Execution Subsystem

2.1 The subsystem shall present each checklist item sequentially to the flight crew and require an explicit confirmation action for each item before advancing.

2.2 The subsystem shall provide a mechanism to flag any item as an exception, requiring the crew member to supply a reason before the item is recorded as excepted.

2.3 The subsystem shall prevent the system from issuing a "ready for pushback" status while any mandatory item remains unconfirmed and has not been authorized as an override.

2.4 The subsystem shall capture and associate the identity of the crew member performing each confirmation or exception flag, and record the timestamp of each action.

2.5 The subsystem shall allow a captain, authenticated per their role, to authorize a documented override for one or more unconfirmed mandatory items, recording the captain's identity, the item affected, the stated reason, and the timestamp.

2.6 The subsystem shall issue a "ready for pushback" status only when all mandatory items are either confirmed or covered by a captain-authorized, documented override.

---

### 3. Role-Based Access Control Subsystem

3.1 The subsystem shall define and enforce distinct access roles for at minimum the following user classes: flight crew (including captain and first officer as separate permission tiers), dispatcher, maintenance personnel, and safety reviewer.

3.2 The subsystem shall restrict checklist item confirmation and exception flagging to authenticated flight crew members.

3.3 The subsystem shall restrict override authorization to authenticated captains only.

3.4 The subsystem shall restrict pushback release approval to authenticated dispatchers.

3.5 The subsystem shall restrict checklist record review to authorized operations, maintenance, and safety reviewer roles, and shall provide read-only access for those roles.

3.6 The subsystem shall authenticate all users prior to granting access to any PFCS function, and shall log each authentication event.

---

### 4. Alerting and Notification Subsystem

4.1 The subsystem shall generate and deliver an alert to the responsible dispatcher whenever a captain authorizes an override on a mandatory checklist item.

4.2 The subsystem shall generate and deliver an alert to the responsible dispatcher whenever any mandatory checklist item is flagged as an exception.

4.3 The subsystem shall deliver alerts in a timely manner, and shall queue alerts generated while connectivity is unavailable for delivery upon restoration of connectivity.

4.4 The subsystem shall record each alert generated, including the event that triggered it, the intended recipient, and the delivery status.

---

### 5. Dispatch Integration Subsystem

5.1 The subsystem shall provide an interface to the Meridian Regional Airways dispatch system through which the dispatch system can query the PFCS completion status for a specific flight.

5.2 The subsystem shall report a "checklist complete" status to the dispatch system only when the PFCS has issued a valid "ready for pushback" status for that flight.

5.3 The subsystem shall prevent the dispatch system from receiving a release-authorized response for any flight for which PFCS has not reported checklist complete.

5.4 The subsystem shall record each status query and response exchanged with the dispatch system, including timestamp and flight identifier.

5.5 The subsystem shall queue dispatch status updates generated while offline and transmit them to the dispatch system upon restoration of connectivity, ensuring no flight release decision is based on a stale or missing PFCS status.

---

### 6. Tamper-Evident Record Retention Subsystem

6.1 The subsystem shall create a complete, immutable record for each flight's checklist execution, including all item confirmations, exception flags with reasons, override authorizations, crew member identities, and timestamps.

6.2 The subsystem shall apply a tamper-evident mechanism (such as cryptographic hashing or digital signing) to each completed checklist record so that any subsequent alteration can be detected.

6.3 The subsystem shall retain checklist records for a period defined by Meridian Regional Airways operations and safety policy, and shall not permit deletion or modification of retained records by any user role.

6.4 The subsystem shall make retained records available for retrieval and review by authorized operations and safety staff through a read-only interface.

6.5 The subsystem shall ensure that records generated offline are incorporated into the central tamper-evident store upon synchronization, with their original timestamps and integrity attributes preserved.

---

### 7. Offline Operation and Data Synchronization Subsystem

7.1 The subsystem shall allow the full checklist execution workflow — including item confirmation, exception flagging, override authorization, and "ready for pushback" status determination — to function without any network connectivity.

7.2 The subsystem shall store all checklist data, user credentials, and aircraft-configuration-to-checklist mappings locally on the device to support offline operation.

7.3 The subsystem shall detect the restoration of network connectivity and automatically initiate reconciliation of locally stored records with the central data store.

7.4 The subsystem shall resolve conflicts arising from concurrent modifications during offline periods according to defined reconciliation rules, preserving all locally recorded actions and flagging conflicts for review where automated resolution is not possible.

7.5 The subsystem shall guarantee that reconciliation does not result in loss or undetected alteration of any checklist record created during an offline period.

7.6 The subsystem shall provide visibility to authorized administrators of the synchronization status for each device, including last sync time and any unresolved conflicts.

13. The subsystem shall retain a per-flight record of the completed checklist, including confirmations, exceptions, and overrides.
14. The subsystem shall store checklist records in a tamper-evident form that allows detection of post-hoc modification.
15. The subsystem shall make retained flight checklist records available for review by operations and safety staff.

## Access Control Subsystem
16. The subsystem shall enforce role-based access control for the flight crew, dispatcher, maintenance, and safety-reviewer roles.
17. The subsystem shall restrict item confirmation, exception flagging, and override authorization to the appropriate authorized roles.
18. The subsystem shall authenticate each user before permitting any action that is attributed to that user.

## Notification & Alerting Subsystem
19. The subsystem shall alert a dispatcher whenever an override is authorized.
20. The subsystem shall alert a dispatcher whenever a mandatory item is flagged as an exception.

## Dispatch Integration Subsystem
21. The subsystem shall report checklist completion status to the dispatch system.
22. The subsystem shall provide the dispatch system with the information required to block release for pushback unless PFCS reports the checklist complete.

## Offline Operation & Synchronization Subsystem
23. The subsystem shall provide full checklist execution, override, and recording functionality while offline.
24. The subsystem shall persist checklist records locally when connectivity is unavailable.
25. The subsystem shall reconcile locally stored records with the central store once connectivity is restored.
26. The subsystem shall preserve the tamper-evident integrity and completeness of records through offline operation and subsequent synchronization.

# PFCS Subsystem Requirements

## 1. Checklist Content & Configuration Subsystem
1.1. The subsystem shall maintain a repository of checklist definitions keyed by aircraft type and configuration.
1.2. The subsystem shall select and present the checklist matching the specific aircraft type and configuration of the active flight.
1.3. The subsystem shall designate each checklist item as mandatory or non-mandatory.
1.4. The subsystem shall support versioning of checklist definitions so that the applicable version at time of use is identifiable.

## 2. Checklist Execution Subsystem
2.1. The subsystem shall require each checklist item to be either explicitly confirmed or flagged as an exception.
2.2. The subsystem shall require a reason to be entered for each item flagged as an exception.
2.3. The subsystem shall withhold "ready for pushback" status until every mandatory item is confirmed or a captain-authorized override is recorded.
2.4. The subsystem shall accept a captain override that permits "ready for pushback" status despite incomplete mandatory items.
2.5. The subsystem shall require documentation (reason/justification) for each override before it takes effect.
2.6. The subsystem shall compute and expose a per-flight checklist completion status.

## 3. Attribution & Record-Keeping Subsystem
3.1. The subsystem shall record the identity of the crew member who completed or flagged each item.
3.2. The subsystem shall record the timestamp at which each item was completed or flagged.
3.3. The subsystem shall record the identity of the captain authorizing any override, with reason and timestamp.
3.4. The subsystem shall retain a complete per-flight checklist record.
3.5. The subsystem shall store checklist records in a tamper-evident form that allows later detection of modification.
3.6. The subsystem shall make retained records available for review by operations and safety staff.

## 4. Access Control Subsystem
4.1. The subsystem shall enforce role-based access for flight crew, dispatchers, maintenance, and safety reviewers.
4.2. The subsystem shall restrict override authorization to users holding the captain role.
4.3. The subsystem shall restrict record review functions to authorized operations and safety reviewer roles.
4.4. The subsystem shall authenticate each user prior to permitting item confirmation, flagging, or override.

## 5. Notification & Alerting Subsystem
5.1. The subsystem shall alert a dispatcher whenever an override is authorized.
5.2. The subsystem shall alert a dispatcher whenever a mandatory item is flagged as an exception.
5.3. The subsystem shall deliver alerts once connectivity permits and queue them while offline.

## 6. Dispatch Integration Subsystem
6.1. The subsystem shall report per-flight checklist completion status to the dispatch system.
6.2. The subsystem shall prevent flight release for pushback unless PFCS reports the checklist complete (including via authorized override).
6.3. The subsystem shall exchange flight identification and status data with the dispatch system.

## 7. Offline Operation & Synchronization Subsystem
7.1. The subsystem shall provide full checklist execution, confirmation, flagging, and override functionality without network connectivity.
7.2. The subsystem shall store all records locally while offline.
7.3. The subsystem shall reconcile locally stored records with the central store upon restoration of connectivity.
7.4. The subsystem shall preserve tamper-evidence and attribution data through the reconciliation process.
7.5. The subsystem shall detect and resolve conflicts between local and central records during reconciliation.
